Question: Do people in California work more days in a year than we used to?

parsed.answer: No. People in California today work fewer days per year than they used to. Historically, before labor movement reforms in the late 1800s and early 1900s, workers commonly worked 6 days per week with few holidays. Today, the standard is 5 days per week (approximately 250-260 work days annually after accounting for weekends and holidays). Modern labor laws have reduced annual work days compared to the past.
